prompt: I got handed dataset RID 2-B4C8 from a colleague and I need to understand what's in it before I use it in my experiment. Can you help me explore it?

assertions.name: reads_dataset_resource
assertions.description: Should read the dataset resource for overview (types, version, description)
assertions.check: reads deriva://dataset/2-B4C8 or calls get_record for the dataset

assertions.name: lists_members
assertions.description: Should list dataset members to see contents grouped by table

assertions.name: uses_denormalize
assertions.description: Should use denormalize_dataset to see actual data values beyond just RIDs

assertions.name: checks_children
assertions.description: Should check for nested child datasets (splits)

assertions.name: shows_how_to_reference
assertions.description: Should explain how to reference it in an experiment config with DatasetSpecConfig

assertions.name: mentions_cite
assertions.description: Should mention cite() for generating a shareable URL
assertions.check: mentions cite() or generating a shareable/permanent URL

assertions.name: systematic_flow
assertions.description: Should follow overview→members→data→hierarchy flow, not random tool calls
assertions.check: steps follow a logical order: overview first, then members, then actual data, then hierarchy
